item.title: Earthquake is devastating blow to Venezuela at time of uncertainty
item.description: The twin quakes come less than six months after Venezuela's then-leader Nicolás Maduro was seized by US forces.

item.title: People flee as building collapses on Caracas outskirts
item.description: The capital and its surrounding areas have seen some of the worst damage from the 7.2 and 7.5 quakes, which hit a minute apart.

item.title: France warns even young people's health at risk as Europe's heatwave shifts east
item.description: Temperatures in Germany could hit 40C in some areas while French officials say deaths linked to the heat are being seen among young people.

item.title: UN pauses Strait of Hormuz evacuation plan after cargo ship attacked
item.description: The ship was reportedly struck by an "unknown projectile" near Oman but no casualties were reported.

item.title: Women alleging rape and sexual assault in France call to abolish statute of limitations
item.description: More than 50 women are calling for the abolition of the statute of limitations which they say has prevented them seeking justice.

item.title: Supreme Court allows Trump to end protected status for Haitian and Syrian immigrants
item.description: The ruling opens the path for the Trump administration to deport hundreds of thousands of immigrants who have been living in the US for years.

item.title: Trump asks Congress for billions for Iran war, after tension with Republicans
item.description: But the budget faces an uphill battle as the president spars with some members of his own party over the issue.

item.title: Families lay flowers on barbed wire barricade on anniversary of deadly Kenya protests
item.description: Protesters demand justice for the dozens killed during the 2024 demonstrations and last year's anniversary protests.

item.title: Ryanair says it will reluctantly not charge parents to sit next to children
item.description: The airline had typically charged adults a fee of £8 each way to sit with their young children.

item.title: Oil price falls back to pre-Iran war levels
item.description: Signs that traffic through the key Strait of Hormuz shipping route is gradually resuming has helped to push the oil price down.

item.title: Teens who hacked TfL were known to police years before cyber-attack
item.description: Owen Flowers and Thalha Jubair were convicted for their roles in the attack, which led to large costs for Transport for London.

item.title: The abundant but expensive energy source that's under your feet
item.description: Start-ups are taking fresh approaches to geothermal energy, but will the economics work?

item.title: Apple hikes some prices by nearly 20% while Xbox raises console cost
item.description: Apple said it had "never seen a component price increase this much, this quickly".

item.title: IBM hails new 'block of flats' design breakthrough for ultra tiny chips
item.description: IBM says it has created the world's first known chip tech below 1 nanometre - but it will be some time before it's ready for production.

item.title: GTA 6 - all you need to know about Rockstar's blockbuster game
item.description: Rockstar's sixth game in the franchise is set to be the biggest game release of the year.
